senses.definition: to quickly browse through the pages of a book, magazine, or other document without reading it thoroughly

examples.x: She flicked through the novel to find her favorite passage.
While waiting at the doctor's office, he idly flicked through a magazine.
He sat down in the library and began to flick through the reference books for research.
Before the meeting, she quickly flicked through the documents to refresh her memory.
Instead of reading it thoroughly, he preferred to flick through the newspaper for headlines.

senses.definition: to quickly look through the pages of a book or magazine without reading it all

examples.x: She liked to flip through fashion magazines for inspiration.
While waiting at the dentist's office, he flipped through a travel guidebook.
In the library, he decided to flip through several reference books to find relevant information.
The student would often flip through textbooks to review before exams.
When researching, she would flip through multiple sources to gather a variety of perspectives.

senses.definition: to carefully look at or review something by going over it again

examples.x: I like to go through my checklist before a trip to ensure I haven't forgotten anything.
She prefers to go through her notes before taking the exam.
Students are encouraged to go through the textbook to grasp the concepts.
The conductor asked the orchestra to go through the musical piece once more.
The coach asked the team to go through the game strategy one more time.

senses.definition: to quickly read or examine something

examples.x: The librarian looked through the catalog to find the book I was requesting.
I will look through my notes before taking the test.
I'm going to look through my emails before starting my workday.
The detective looked through the crime scene photos to identify any potential clues.
The teacher is looking through the students' notebooks to check their progress.

senses.definition: to carefully search a place in order to find something specific

examples.x: Amidst the mess, he carefully picks through, looking for any sign of the lost ring.
At the market, she picks through the bins to find the freshest produce.
In old bookstores, he loves to pick through in hopes of finding a rare edition.
She always picks through the sale racks, looking for a bargain.
Archaeologists pick through ancient sites to uncover artifacts.

senses.definition: to carefully read something from start to finish, often with the purpose of identifying errors

examples.x: She read through the manuscript, highlighting grammatical errors and suggesting improvements.
They read through the proposal, evaluating its feasibility and identifying any areas of concern.
The editor read through the article, providing feedback and suggestions for enhancing the content.

senses.definition: to carefully review and sort through a substantial amount of material

examples.x: The detective spent hours sifting through the evidence at the crime scene.
The team is currently sifting through the applications to shortlist candidates.
The researcher sifted through the data, identifying key trends.
The archivist is continuously sifting through historical records for preservation.

senses.definition: to quickly read a text without studying the details

examples.x: I only had a few minutes, so I decided to skim through the report to grasp the main points.
She skims through the newspaper every morning to stay updated on current events.
The professor advised us to skim through the textbook before the lecture for a general understanding of the topics.
In preparation for the meeting, he quickly skimmed through the agenda to see the key discussion points.
Instead of reading the entire novel, she opted to skim through the chapters to get a sense of the storyline.

senses.definition: to carefully consider all aspects of a situation or decision

examples.x: She always thinks through her options before making a choice.
Before signing the contract, make sure to think through all the terms and conditions.

senses.definition: to carefully examine a problem or situation in order to reach a solution

examples.x: We have to work through these issues before we can move forward with the project.
It’s a complex situation but we’ll work through it step by step.
She patiently worked through the software manual to understand its features.
He saw a psychologist to help him work through his depression.
